feedback.claim: The spec does not define behavior when reading watch_path fails (file missing, permission denied, I/O error). Implementations will diverge and operators cannot predict or reason about what the system will do.
feedback.evidence: Section 'Subsequent Cycles' step 1 says 'Read the file bytes from watch_path' but specifies no error handling for read failures. Section 'Failure Handling' only addresses notification send failures, not file read failures.
feedback.invariant_violated: Operators must be able to predict and observe system behavior under all runtime conditions
feedback.recommended_change: Add a 'Read Failure Handling' subsection specifying: (1) the system MUST log the read error to failure_log_path using the same JSONL format with an appropriate error key, (2) the system MUST NOT update sha256_previous, and (3) the system MUST continue polling on the normal schedule.
feedback.severity_rationale: A file read failure is a common operational event (file rotated, permissions changed, path misconfigured). Without defined behavior, operators cannot write runbooks or set alerts, and implementations are non-deterministic.

feedback.claim: The failure_log_path file has no defined size limit or rotation policy, making disk exhaustion a production risk with no specified mitigation.
feedback.evidence: The 'Failure Handling' section specifies MUST append to failure_log_path with no mention of size bounds, rotation, or truncation behavior.
feedback.invariant_violated: Operator-managed resources must have defined lifecycle bounds
feedback.recommended_change: Add a note or configuration field specifying a maximum file size or rotation policy, or explicitly state that log rotation is the operator's responsibility and out of scope for this system.
feedback.severity_rationale: Unbounded append-only log files are a well-known operational hazard. The spec should either bound the growth or explicitly delegate responsibility to the operator.

feedback.claim: poll_interval_seconds has no defined minimum value. A value of 0 or negative would cause a tight polling loop or undefined behavior, giving operators no spec-level protection.
feedback.evidence: The Configuration section lists poll_interval_seconds: 60 as an example but specifies no valid range or minimum. The Behavior section uses the value without asserting it is positive.
feedback.invariant_violated: Configuration parameters must have validated bounds to prevent degenerate operational states
feedback.recommended_change: Add a constraint: poll_interval_seconds MUST be a positive integer greater than or equal to 1.
feedback.severity_rationale: Missing lower bounds on timing parameters are a known operability gap that can cause resource exhaustion or undefined scheduling behavior in degenerate configurations.
